title: Review of Monetary Policy Strategy, Tools, and Communications
summary: The Federal Reserve completed its second periodic review of its monetary policy strategy, tools, and communications in 2025, releasing a revised Statement on Longer-Run Goals and Monetary Policy Strategy on August 22, 2025. The review process included 10 Fed Listens events with various public stakeholders and the Second Thomas Laubach Research Conference, with substantive FOMC discussion at five consecutive meetings. The 2 percent longer-run inflation goal was explicitly not a focus of this review. The revised statement establishes a regular five-year review cycle going forward.
key_points: Federal Reserve completed its second periodic review of monetary policy strategy, tools, and communications in 2025
Revised Statement on Longer-Run Goals and Monetary Policy Strategy released August 22, 2025
The 2% longer-run inflation goal was not a focus of the 2025 review
Review process included 10 Fed Listens events engaging workforce organizations, retirees, unions, small businesses, and low/moderate-income communities
FOMC discussed review topics at five consecutive meetings beginning January 2025, informed by System staff analytical work
Revised statement commits FOMC to regular review of monetary policy strategy roughly every five years
why_it_matters: This review establishes the Federal Reserve's operational framework for policy decisions and communications going forward. The commitment to regular five-year reviews creates predictability for markets regarding how the Fed will update its policy approach. The fact that the 2% inflation target was not revisited signals continuity in the inflation-fighting framework, though any changes to policy communications tools could affect how the market interprets future Fed signals.
macro_context: The review establishes the strategic foundation for how the FOMC pursues its dual mandate of maximum employment and price stability. While the inflation goal remained unchanged, modifications to policy communications tools could affect how market participants interpret future Fed guidance and signals.

issues_found: Source is a homepage/navigation page per CORE RULE 5 — must reject
Actual revised Statement on Longer-Run Goals is contained in separate PDF not included in this source
Source provides overview context but lacks substantive policy content for archival value
No specific policy language, framework changes, or communications modifications are detailed in this page
Source is thin — effectively a table of contents for the review materials

notes: Source is a Federal Reserve homepage/navigation page providing an overview of the 2025 framework review. The substantive revised policy statement is contained in a separate PDF document (linked but not included in this crawl). This source provides good contextual information about the review process and structure but lacks the specific policy language that would be needed for a comprehensive archival record. The specific changes to the Statement on Longer-Run Goals and any modifications to policy communications tools are not detailed here.
